supply_chain.dependencies: Highly dependent on commercial airlines, ocean carriers, and ground transportation providers for cargo capacity. Non-asset-based model means reliance on third-party carriers for all transportation services.
risks: Carrier financial stability and capacity constraints
Volatile fuel prices impacting carrier rates
Red Sea security disruptions affecting ocean transit times
Sudden carrier rate increases with little notice

regulatory_risks: Transportation Security Administration (TSA) security regulations for indirect air carriers
Federal Maritime Commission (FMC) economic regulations for Ocean Transportation Intermediaries
Customs and Border Protection (CBP) licensing and audit requirements
Air Cargo Advance Screening (ACAS) compliance
Customs Trade Partnership Against Terrorism (CTPAT) requirements
Authorized Economic Operator (AEO) programs in multiple countries
Foreign ownership restrictions on customs brokerage in some jurisdictions
Anti-corruption laws including U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act
Trade and exchange control laws and embargoes
Anti-boycott laws and regulations
Environmental regulations on emissions and climate change
EU Emissions Trading System requirements
FuelEU Maritime initiative
Data privacy regulations
Employment and compensation regulations across jurisdictions
Cargo security regulations globally
Changes in tariffs and trade restrictions
Import and export regulations
Tax regulations including transfer pricing rules
OECD Pillar Two minimum 15% tax rate implementation

sanction_risks: Compliance with trade sanctions laws and regulations
Executive Orders imposing embargoes and sanctions
Restrictions on operations in hostile locations
Red Sea disruptions due to Middle East conflicts
US-China trade tensions and tariff increases
Potential tariffs on imports from Canada and Mexico
